situation: audit-2026-07-02-006 finding M-002 (priority 7, route-fix): drillDownToPreCheckTask() serially traverses jobs via a1 CLI to find the '发布准入' task. After successful drill-down, the task_id was only persisted indirectly via the final updateEvent call at L213. If that updateEvent failed (network blip), next poll cycle would redundantly re-drill — wasteful (multiple a1 CLI calls) and slow (>5s per drill). Audit report recommended immediate separate updateEvent after drill-down success.
action: Added an immediate payload-only updateEvent({task_id}) call right after drillDownToPreCheckTask() returns in main(), wrapped in try/catch so failure is non-fatal (logged as WARN, falls back to prior behavior of re-drilling next cycle). This ensures cache persistence is decoupled from the later full-payload updateEvent that carries check_items/summary/poll_time. Single-file change, no API surface modification.
outcome_evidence: Commit e56c909 landed on auto/opt-prod-deploy-1782970212, pushed to origin. opt-worktree.sh confirmed route-fix (reused existing prod-deploy worktree, no direction-shift). Diff adds 9 lines (try/catch block + comment) after L169 drillDownToPreCheckTask() call.
next_suggestion: Next pending finding by priority: L-001 (getHeaders/getBaseUrl dedup across task-client+event-client, low severity but pairs well with L-003 already merged). Alternatively AE2-M-001 (Windows paths in memory files, autonomous-engine project — different area, would open new worktree).
